text:
te Mandi and Gulika.
When the duration of the day is thirty ghatis or twelve
hours, Mandi appears at the end of26, 22, 18, 14, 10, 6 and 2
.ghatis counted from Sunday onwards. 1f the duration is more
or less than thirty ghatis, we have to increase or reduce these
proportionately. If the birth is at night and if the duration of
the night is thirty ghatis, Mandi appears at the end of 10, 6, 2,
26, 22, 18 and 14 ghatis counted from Sunday onwards.
For Gulika divide the duration of the day or night into
eight equal parts. For a day birth count from that day on
wards. The end of Saturday's part is that of Gulika. For a
night birth count from the fifth day (from the day of birth);
and the end of Saturday's part gives the position of Gulika.
These calculations may fix the lagna. But they do not pro
vide any clue to the correct time of birth.
V. According to some authorities the position of Prana
pada enables us to fix the time of birth. The concept of Prana
